authoritative_sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual, Sector 56 and Subsector 561 Administrative and Support Services
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Census Bureau and Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The NAICS manual is the official U.S. industry classification baseline. It defines administrative and support services as routine support activities for day-to-day operations and separates support services from strategic management and public administration.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Named-industry scope, support-service boundary, and artifact vocabulary
used_for: refusal when intake becomes client management or single-service specialist work

authoritative_sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual, Industry Group 5614 Business Support Services and 56142 Telephone Call Centers
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Census Bureau and Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NAICS 5614 and 56142 are official industry definitions for ongoing routine business support, document preparation, answering calls, relaying messages, taking orders, and representing client services without owning the represented products or services.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Business support intake channel, work-type classification, and represented-client boundary review
used_for: document preparation and business support service scope
boundary against approving or performing the client service itself

authoritative_sources.title: Administrative and Support Services: NAICS 561
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: BLS is a federal labor-statistics authority. Its industry profile confirms NAICS 561 service families and public terminology for administrative and support services used to constrain the overlay's named industry.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Industry service-family confirmation and terminology normalization

authoritative_sources.title: NAPCS Product List for NAICS 5614: Business Support Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Census product list is an official public reference for business support service outputs, grounding examples such as call center, message, document, mail, copy, and business service center work that create intake records and handoff artifacts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Service product identification and artifact mapping
used_for: proof that the industry label materially changes the intake artifact set

authoritative_sources.title: Contact Center Technologies
authoritative_sources.publisher: Digital.gov and U.S. General Services Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Digital.gov/GSA guidance is an official public operating reference for contact centers. It supports IVR, call routing, skills-based routing, business rules, source and destination routing, and routing to the right agent for intake and handoff control.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Channel intake, skill or business-rule routing, and next-owner selection

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 10002:2018 Quality management - Customer satisfaction - Guidelines for complaints handling in organizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO 10002 is a recognized international standard for complaints handling. It provides a vendor-neutral public baseline for receipt, acknowledgement, tracking, escalation, evidence, and improvement of complaint-like intake workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Complaint or service issue intake, acknowledgement, tracking, and escalation
used_for: escalation when complaint authority exceeds intake ownership

authoritative_sources.title: FAR Part 37 - Service Contracting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAR Part 37 is official U.S. service-contracting guidance. It anchors SOW, performance-based service, and surveillance concepts used as a public baseline for SOW-bound administrative service intake while requiring tenant-specific contracts for final routing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Service-contract scope and SOW prerequisite review
used_for: intake refusal when request falls outside service scope
boundary between intake status and service acceptance or approval

authoritative_sources.title: Universal Electronic Records Management Requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. National Archives and Records Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the U.S. federal records authority. The Universal ERM Requirements define baseline business needs for electronic records capture, maintenance and use, disposal, transfer, metadata, and reporting, which directly constrain intake evidence and handoff traceability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Intake record capture, metadata, retention context, and handoff evidence

authoritative_sources.title: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations, SP 800-53 Rev. 5
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 is an authoritative public control catalog for information systems and organizations. It supports access control, identification, audit and accountability, privacy, and system-integrity controls for systems that intake and route administrative work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Identity, access, audit, privacy, and system-control review

workflow_stages.name: Administrative support channel intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm channel, requester, client or business unit, service family, service catalog item, SOW scope, requested outcome, SLA clock, and whether the request is routine administrative support, business support service work, complaint-like intake, or adjacent specialist authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite, identity, eligibility, authorization, and access validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate required intake fields, requester identity, account or client record, authorization matrix, eligibility or service prerequisites, required attachments, access role, duplicate status, record location, retention context, and retrieval hooks before accepting work.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Acceptance, rejection, pend, duplicate, or misroute disposition
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether the intake can be accepted, rejected, pended, marked duplicate, or routed elsewhere based on the service catalog, SOW, routing matrix, queue rules, owner map, evidence bundle, and source hierarchy.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Pre-execution routing and handoff package assembly
workflow_stages.description: Select the next owner and required artifact set, prepare routing summary, handoff status, SLA and blocker state, evidence links, and current owner accountability before downstream execution starts.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Conflict, missing-authority, and adjacent-lane escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop when SOW, service catalog, access policy, tenant policy, public guidance, source records, or operating practice conflict, or when the request requires legal, HR, finance, compliance, privacy, security, safety, client approval, or downstream execution authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: case, ticket, call, message, webform, email, referral, reservation, work-order, or intake form record with channel, timestamp, source, priority, SLA state, and requested outcome
client master record, account or requester identity record, business unit, service family, jurisdiction, program, facility, and contract context
client service catalog, statement of work, service-level commitment, intake SOP, routing matrix, escalation matrix, approval threshold, and exception policy
work-type taxonomy, queue state, owner assignment, duplicate check, IVR skill route, transcript or message summary, call disposition, and prior handoff history
authorization record, access role, eligibility or prerequisite checklist, required attachments, source document packet, e-signature or approval evidence, and record location
privacy label, confidentiality restriction, retention schedule, legal hold flag, access restriction, and audit logging requirement
intake disposition note, prerequisite checklist, routing or escalation summary, blocked-dependency note, handoff status summary, and source-conflict escalation packet
systems_of_record: CRM or client master data system
BPM, workflow, or shared-services queue system
contact center, IVR, ACD, call recording, transcript, or message-routing platform
contract, SOW, and service catalog repository
identity, access, and entitlement management system
GRC, audit log, or evidence repository
communication archive for email, chat, SMS, and correspondence records

allowed: Classify incoming administrative support and business-services work as in-scope, out-of-scope, duplicate, misrouted, blocked, or ready for intake.
Request or validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, source context, requester identity, authorization evidence, access constraints, and intake prerequisites.
Accept, reject, pend, or route incoming work based on documented prerequisites, service eligibility, SOW scope, service catalog rules, queue states, access rules, and routing matrices.
Determine the correct next owner and required artifact set for pre-execution handoffs.
Prepare intake disposition notes, prerequisite checklists, routing summaries, blocked-dependency notes, and handoff status summaries without overstating authority.
escalate: Required tenant facts, source records, service catalog item, SOW scope, authorization evidence, access model, queue owner, retrieval hook, or handoff context is missing.
Requester identity, client record, authorization, eligibility, access, service catalog, SOW, queue state, or source hierarchy conflicts materially.
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams or duplicate queue items could cause dropped or repeated work.
Public source, tenant policy, client SOW, access rule, retention requirement, or operating practice conflict materially.
The request requires legal, licensed, policy-override, financial-approval, HR, privacy, security, safety-critical, client approval, or downstream execution authority.
refuse: Skip prerequisite, identity, authorization, eligibility, access, duplicate, or handoff-context checks to accelerate work.

Approve downstream work, grant access, waive eligibility, approve policy exceptions, release payment, decide HR action, provide legal interpretation, or resolve compliance, privacy, security, or safety authority in-lane.

Accept or route work as ready when required intake data, SOW scope, service catalog item, source record, authorization evidence, record location, queue owner, or source hierarchy is missing or conflicting.
Treat Administrative Support And Business Services as permission to run the client's complete business process or to absorb a more specialized adjacent lane.

domain_failure_modes: the pack stays generic and fails to encode NAICS 561 support-service boundaries, contact-center and business support terminology, SOW/service catalog dependencies, queue ownership, and cross-client confidentiality controls
intake accepts work without required client ID, service catalog item, SOW scope, requester identity, authorization, access role, attachment, queue owner, or record location
the lane routes work around eligibility, authorization, access, retention, privacy, duplicate, or handoff-context controls to keep the queue moving
contact-center or shared-services intake is mistaken for authority to approve or perform the represented client service

ownership ambiguity across document preparation, mailroom, contact center, shared services, records, compliance, finance, HR, IT access, privacy, or client approval queues causes dropped or duplicated work
conflicting systems or policies are synthesized into confident acceptance or routing without source-of-truth resolution
tenant-specific SOW, service catalog, queue taxonomy, routing matrix, access model, or owner map is missing and the lane guesses instead of returning to orchestrator

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Missing prerequisite or required intake data
Request routed to the wrong lane before execution starts
Attempt to bypass access, eligibility, or authorization checks
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific SOW scope, service catalog taxonomy, routing matrix, queue ownership, authorization rules, and access controls vary materially by client, jurisdiction, service model, and system configuration.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline establishes industry scope, intake and routing concepts, and evidence controls. Runtime must retrieve tenant SOW, service catalog, routing matrix, policies, source hierarchy, systems, and owner map before acting on a specific request.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Administrative support intake can overlap with contact-center operations, back-office processing, records management, compliance, finance, HR, privacy, security, legal operations, and client approval lanes.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack resolves this by requiring service catalog and adjacent owner mapping. If ownership cannot be identified, the lane returns to orchestrator instead of absorbing adjacent work.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec package because tenant-specific variation is converted into explicit retrieval, refusal, and handoff requirements.

The research baseline combines NAICS 561 named-industry guidance, business support service product references, public contact-center routing guidance, complaint-handling workflow guidance, service-contracting boundaries, NARA records requirements, and NIST system controls.
The lane remains specification-based and does not claim retained implementation behavior.
